pi_items.source: File inspection: /examples/equest_env/data/4StoreyBuilding.inp contains definition of 4-storey commercial office building with 5 Ground Floor zones (G.S11, G.E12, G.N13, G.W14, G.C15) and multiple perimeter/core spaces
File inspection: 4StoreyBuilding.inp lines 79-84 (EWall Construction) and lines 96-101 (Roof Construction); both are CONSTRUCTION type with ABSORPTANCE property
File inspection: 4StoreyBuilding.inp line 82 shows 'ABSORPTANCE = 0.6' for EWall Construction; line 99 shows 'ABSORPTANCE = 0.6' for Roof Construction

National Construction Code (NCC) 2019 (Australia) requires solar absorptance ≤ 0.45 for roof compliance via Deemed to Satisfy pathway. This value corresponds to solar reflectance of 0.55 (1 - 0.45 = 0.55), consistent with high-performance cool surface standards. Also referenced in DOE-2.2 context where default absorptance is 0.70.
File inspection: 4StoreyBuilding.inp contains exact system definitions at lines 2602, 2643, 2684, 2725, 2766 with these exact names

File inspection: 4StoreyBuilding.inp contains 20 instances of 'DRYBULB-LIMIT = 70' across all HVAC systems (lines 2412, 2453, 2494, 2535, 2576, 2617, 2658, 2699, 2740, 2781, 2822, 2863, 2904, 2945, 2986, 3027, 3068, 3109, 3150, 3191)

HVAC economizer control standard: 65°F dry-bulb setpoint is a documented control threshold used as both a high-limit shutoff point and within enthalpy-based control strategies (referenced in MEP Academy, Trane, and PNNL documentation). This is lower than the common 55°F threshold, enabling more aggressive free cooling.

Verifier logic (verifier.py line 112) requires 'sim_file_is_new' (timestamp check) as a pass criterion. Export script (export_result.ps1 lines 36-50) explicitly checks for .SIM files and validates their modification time is newer than task start timestamp.

privileged_info_summary: The 4StoreyBuilding is a real commercial office building model in BDL format with baseline solar absorptance of 0.6 for both exterior walls (EWall Construction) and roof (Roof Construction). The target absorptance of 0.45 matches the National Construction Code (NCC) 2019 cool roof standard requiring absorptance ≤ 0.45. The model contains exactly five ground-floor packaged single-zone HVAC systems labeled Sys1 (PSZ) with location codes G.S11, G.E12, G.N13, G.W14, and G.C15 (South, East, North, West, Core). All systems currently have a dry-bulb economizer limit (DRYBULB-LIMIT) of 70°F, which the task requires to reduce to 65°F—a documented threshold in HVAC economizer control strategies. DOE-2.2 simulation output is required to validate the changes.
